subject.title: Biology
subject.description:
**IB Biology** is a **Group 4** subject in the **International Baccalaureate Diploma Programme (IBDP)**. It explores **four interconnected themes: Unity and Diversity, Form and Function, Interaction and Interdependence, and Continuity and Change**, with a strong focus on **experimental work, scientific inquiry, data analysis, and practical investigation skills**.

The course is available at **Standard Level (SL, 150 teaching hours)** and **Higher Level (HL, 240 teaching hours)**, with **first assessment in 2025**.

### Assessment at a Glance

| Assessment | Format | SL | HL | Weighting |
|------------|--------|----|----|-----------|
| **Paper 1** | Multiple-choice and data-based questions | 1 hour 20 min | 2 hours | 36% |
| **Paper 2** | Short and extended response questions | 1 hour 30 min | 2 hours 30 min | 44% |
| **Scientific Investigation** | Individual research project | 10 hours | 10 hours | 20% |

strengths: Clear and precise formulation of the research question with explicit hypotheses
Comprehensive background explanation with accurate technical terminology
Well-organized table of controlled variables demonstrating thorough planning
weaknesses: Omission of exact ClinicalTrials.gov date range and version limits reproducibility
Lack of justification for single-registry reliance and heterogeneous follow-up period consolidation
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: While the investigationâs focus is exceptionally well communicated and the research question sharply defined (top-band qualities), minor methodological details are missing. These omissions impede full reproducibility and thus the work falls short of the very highest level. A score of 4/6 reflects this strong but not flawless performance.

strengths: Frequent and generally relevant citation of primary trials and reviews
Accurate and consistent use of specialist terminology once corrected
weaknesses: Some applications of source material are partially effective or inferred rather than directly reported
Minor inaccuracies in statistical concept explanations and use of less authoritative sources
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The student demonstrates a good command of terminology (5-6 band) but the application of source material shows partial effectiveness and occasional inaccuracies, placing the work solidly in the mid band. A score of 3/6 appropriately balances strengths and weaknesses.

strengths: Logical progression from background through statistical analysis to conclusion
Good relevance of the chi-square analysis to the research question with clear linkage
Well-supported evaluation of methodological limitations and concrete improvement suggestions
weaknesses: Minor inconsistencies such as treating categorical data as normal and absence of degrees-of-freedom reporting
No sensitivity or subgroup analyses to probe robustness
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The critical thinking is solidly in the mid-band: arguments are coherent and evaluation is well supported (7â9 band descriptors), but small gaps prevent it from reaching excellence. A score of 5/12 acknowledges the quality of analysis and evaluation while reflecting those minor inconsistencies.

strengths: Clear and appropriate overall structure with comprehensive table of contents
Consistent application of formatting conventions (title page, page numbering, captions)
weaknesses: Inconsistent percentage formatting, incomplete paragraph and table entries
Formatting of equations and attribution errors remain
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Presentation is strong and meets most top-band requirements, but a cluster of minor formatting and completeness issues prevents a perfect score. A mark of 3/4 accurately reflects this high but imperfect standard.

strandSelections.reasoning: The report follows the conventional EE structure (abstract omitted per new guidelines), with well-labelled sections, logical sequencing, a detailed table of contents and appropriate integration of figures and tables.  Minor issuesâoccasional lengthy subsections and some redundancyâdo not impede comprehension.  The structure is therefore clear and appropriate throughout, aligned with the top 3-4 band; a score of 4 reflects strong, though not flawless, organisation.

Formatting conventions required by the EE guide are met: title page, word count (3 643), sub-headings, justified text, numbered pages, tables centred and captioned, figures labelled, and references in a consistent style.  Some lengthy URLs are not truncated and a few line-break artefacts appear, but these are minor.  Overall, layout considerations are correctly applied, fulfilling the 3-4 band level with a strong score of 4.

The argument progresses logically: background â precise RQ â methodological design â data analysis â conclusion â implications.  Each section builds on the previous, and the line of reasoning remains clear.  Minor coherence gaps (e.g., treating categorical data as ânormalâ, some over-interpretation of percentages) introduce slight inconsistencies, preventing the highest band.  The essay thus fits the âclear and coherent argument with minor inconsistenciesâ description, meriting a 7 in the 7-9 band.

The student presents a biomedical topic of clear significanceâ the influence of pathological complete response (pCR) on overall survival in HER2-positive breast-cancer patientsâ and explains it with impressive depth. The introduction provides epidemiological context, molecular background (ERBB2 over-expression, PI3K/MAPK pathways), diagnostic criteria, and therapeutic approaches, all supported by relevant citations and explanatory figures produced by the student. Technical terms (e.g., neoadjuvant vs adjuvant chemotherapy, taxanes, monoclonal antibodies) are defined, and visual aids (BioRender diagrams) help the reader grasp complex mechanisms. Overall, the communication is accurate, comprehensive and accessible, fully satisfying the top-band descriptor.

The essay draws on a range of primary trials, reviews and authoritative databases; citations are frequent and generally relevant, and the literature is integrated into background, analysis and discussion.  However, some applications are only partially effective: raw counts are sometimes inferred rather than reported; the explanation of chi-square assumptions and interpretation shows minor inaccuracies; and a few sources (e.g., popular websites) add limited academic value.  These issues place the work solidly in the 3â4 band, with a high-band score of 4.

The student conducts a multi-step statistical analysis: converting percentages to absolute frequencies, assessing normality, constructing a 2Ã2 contingency table, and applying an appropriate chi-square test.  Calculations are shown and interpreted, and the analysis is explicitly linked to the research question.  Limitations (heterogeneous follow-up times, drug-regimen variability) are acknowledged, though not fully resolved with sensitivity checks.  The analysis is therefore âgoodâ and clearly relevant but not consistently âexcellentâ, placing it in the 7-9 band with a mid-band score of 8.

The study identifies an appropriate secondary-data, comparative methodology, justified through clear inclusion/exclusion criteria, a rationale for choosing ClinicalTrials.gov, and an explanation of ethical considerations.  Key variables, control measures and limitations are tabulated.  Nevertheless, some methodological detailsâexact search dates, database version, justification for relying on a single registry and for combining heterogeneous follow-up periodsâare missing, which slightly weakens reproducibility.  These minor gaps move the work toward the lower end of the top band, warranting a score of 5 rather than 6.

The discussion weighs strengths, methodological constraints, clinical and ethical implications, and proposes concrete improvements (e.g., broader geographic sampling, matching follow-up periods, standardising drug doses). Conclusions are directly supported by statistical evidence.  Nevertheless, evaluation of certain issuesâsuch as consent in secondary data use and cost-effectivenessâremains brief, and no sensitivity or subgroup analyses are performed to probe robustness.  The work meets the âgood evaluation with well-supported conclusionsâ descriptor, earning a 7 within the 7-9 band.

The research questionââÂ¿En quÃ© medida la presencia o ausencia de Respuesta PatolÃ³gica Completa afecta la tasa de supervivencia generalâ¦?ââis unambiguous, sharply delimited to adult female HER2-positive breast-cancer patients receiving a specific therapeutic regimen.  The student keeps the investigation tightly centred on this focus throughout, returning to it in the analysis, conclusion and discussion.  Objectives, variables and hypotheses are explicitly stated, showing a purposeful and sustained focus that aligns with the 5â6 band requirements.

Throughout the essay, the student deploys specialist biological and biostatistical terminologyâHER2 over-expression, neoadjuvant, pCR, taxanes, Kolmogorov-Smirnov test, chi-square, expected frequenciesâwith accuracy and consistency.  Definitions are usually supplied at first mention, and concepts such as signalling pathways (PI3K/MAPK) and apoptosis are explained correctly.  Minor lapses (e.g., describing pCR as a 'qualitative' rather than binary categorical variable) prevent full marks, but overall use aligns with the 5-6 band, meriting a score of 5.

annotations.comment: The bibliography mixes citation styles and inconsistent formatting; apply a single style guide (e.g., APA 7) consistently across all references.
Percentages in Tabla 2 use inconsistent formats (e.g., '29' vs. '66.7'); standardize to one decimal place for clarity and consistency.
The candidate name field after âCandidato:â is left blank; ensure all required administrative details are completed to meet formatting requirements.
The table of contents is comprehensive and clearly structured, facilitating navigation and demonstrating coherent organization of the report.
The platform is named 'Clinical Trails Gov.'âcorrect to 'ClinicalTrials.gov' to accurately reference the data source.
The description that chi-square value indicates 'porcentaje en el que los valores se alejan del esperado' is misleading; clarify that ÏÂ² measures summed squared deviations, not a percentage.
The ethical and clinical implications are blended in one narrative; separate economic, access, and consent issues into distinct subsections for clearer critical evaluation.
The research question is clearly and precisely formulated, specifying population, intervention, and outcome, which demonstrates a focused and appropriate research issue.
The paragraph ends abruptly with 'mientras que aquellas cÃ©lulas sin'; please complete this sentence to fully describe receptor-negative cell types.
The term 'neoayuyante' appears to be a typographical error; correct to 'neoadyuvante' at first mention and throughout to maintain accuracy in terminology.
OncoLink and Rockymount cancer center blogs are secondary popular sources; replace these with peer-reviewed primary literature for more rigorous support of diagnostic criteria.
SlideShare is a non-peer-reviewed source; replace this with a published article or official guideline detailing the trastuzumab mechanism for stronger academic validity.
The caption for Image 1 attributes the source to Kenhub but notes the figure was created in BioRender; correct the attribution to maintain accuracy.
The methodology rationale omits the exact date range and version of ClinicalTrials.gov used; include these details to enhance reproducibility.
The null and alternative hypotheses are explicitly stated with clear predictions, demonstrating a well-focused and testable research plan.
The dependent variable 'Tasa de Supervivencia General' is described as qualitative; it should be characterized as quantitative (continuous percentage) for correct variable classification.
The table of controlled variables is well-organized, listing each factor, its justification, and control method, demonstrating thorough planning.
The detailed analysis of differing follow-up durations, drug concentrations, and regimens demonstrates strong critical evaluation of methodological limitations.
The description of non-controlled variables ends mid-sentence at 'tiempos se'; complete this explanation to fully address the impact of follow-up duration variability.
Equations are embedded via <math> tags; reformat all mathematics using LaTeX ($$â¦$$) to align with conventions and improve readability.
A KolmogorovâSmirnov test is not appropriate for categorical contingency data; omit this step and directly apply the ÏÂ² test, which does not assume normality of counts.
The worked example for study NOAH clearly demonstrates calculation of frequencies, effectively illustrating the statistical procedure.
The opening sentence of 'Fortalezas' is lengthy and diffuse; consider streamlining to highlight key strengths more directly.
In the limitations table, the 'Mejora' column entries trail off; complete each recommendation so readers can clearly apply suggested improvements.
The 2Ã2 contingency table accurately reflects summed frequencies and aligns directly with the research question, supporting valid statistical testing.
Report the degrees of freedom alongside the ÏÂ² and p-value to fully document the statistical test context and support transparent interpretation.
The personal reflection conveys strong intellectual engagement and authentic motivation, linking research experience to future academic and social commitments.
